key_insight: Open-source LLMs can convert messy longitudinal liver disease notes into structured patient timelines at scale, enabling new cancer-risk research from routine clinical text.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help identify and track chronic liver disease patients at higher risk of liver cancer earlier and more systematically using existing records.
method: Manually annotated 507 reports from 30 patients across radiology, pathology, and transplant assessments.
Benchmarked four open-source LLMs against a regex baseline across 73 extraction tasks.
Used constrained decoding to force structured outputs, then applied the best pipeline to 835 transplant patients.
evidence_signal: Llama 3.3 70B was strongest, with >90% accuracy on 59/73 tasks, >99.9% format adherence under constrained decoding, and 37,125 datapoints extracted from 22,493 reports.
watch_item: Performance was tested in one center’s transplant cohort, so external validation is needed before broader clinical deployment.
paper_title: LLM-based reconstruction of longitudinal clinical trajectories in chronic liver disease.
